title: Satellite snaps amazing 36th birthday pic of Hubble Space Telescope (photo)
summary: A private WorldView Legion 4 Earth-observing satellite captured a photo of the Hubble Space Telescope on April 23, 2026, one day before its 36th anniversary.

title: Why do some starburst galaxies mysteriously shut down? New study provides clues
summary: A new study reveals that post-starburst galaxies shut down star formation primarily due to fuel depletion, though other factors may also play a role.

title: Russia launches 3 tons of cargo to the International Space Station from Kazakhstan (video)
summary: Russia launched a Progress cargo spacecraft carrying 3 tons of supplies to the International Space Station from Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan on April 25. The mission marks another resupply effort to support the orbiting laboratory.

title: How did humanity end up underground in 'Silo'? Apple TV digs deep in new Season 3 teaser (video)
summary: Apple TV released a new teaser for 'Silo' Season 3, exploring the origins of humanity's underground existence. The show's narrative revolves around uncovering the mystery behind the beginning of life in the silo.

title: This X-ray image shows our solar system 'breathing'
summary: The eROSITA spacecraft has created the most precise map of soft X-rays over Earth, revealing a phenomenon driven by solar winds from the sun. This X-ray image illustrates the solar system's dynamic interaction with solar activity.

title: Viltrox AF 50mm f/1.4 Pro FE lens review
summary: The Viltrox AF 50mm f/1.4 Pro FE is a fast standard prime lens designed for astrophotography, offering competitive pricing. It is not directly related to the space industry but is relevant for space imaging applications.

title: How did the USSR win the space race to beat the US to the moon in Apple TV's new 'Star City'?
summary: Apple TV's new series 'Star City' explores how the USSR achieved early space milestones, including beating the US in the space race. The show highlights Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin's historic flight and ideological motivations.

title: Comet PanSTARRS approaches Earth on April 26. Here's how to catch it in satellite imagery this weekend
summary: Comet PanSTARRS will approach Earth on April 26 and can be observed in satellite imagery this weekend. The comet recently survived a close encounter with the sun.

title: I Am Artemis: Peter Rossoni
summary: Peter Rossoni, Orion Artemis II Optical Communications System flight manager, reflects on his childhood inspiration from Apollo missions and his role in NASA's Artemis II mission, enabling communications for astronauts around the Moon in April 2026.

title: The Day of the Trifid Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a detailed image of the Trifid Nebula, 5,000 light-years from Earth, to celebrate the 36th anniversary of its launch. The image was released on April 20, 2026, showcasing vibrant colors reminiscent of an underwater scene.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Missouri Students
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack Hathaway will answer STEM questions from Missouri students during a live Earth-to-space call from the International Space Station on April 30. The event will be streamed on NASA’s Learn With NASA YouTube channel.

title: NASA Celebrates Decade of University Innovation in Aeronautics
summary: NASA's University Leadership Initiative (ULI) has celebrated a decade of fostering aeronautical innovations and training the next generation of aviation professionals. The initiative has supported over 1,100 students across 100 schools, driving breakthroughs in 21st-century air travel.

title: Johnson Leaders Honored by National Space Club & Foundation
summary: Two leaders from NASA's Johnson Space Center were honored by the National Space Club & Foundation for their contributions to human spaceflight. Howard Hu received the Norman L. Baker Astronautics Engineer Award for his technical leadership in human spaceflight efforts.

title: NASA Shares SpaceX Crew-13 Assignments for Space Station Mission
summary: NASA has announced the crew assignments for the SpaceX Crew-13 mission, which will send four astronauts from three space agencies to the International Space Station in mid-September for a long-duration science expedition. The crew includes NASA astronauts Jessica Watkins and Luke Delaney, along with a CSA astronaut.

title: NASA Kennedy Prepares Facility for Roman Space Telescope Arrival
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center is preparing facilities for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for as early as September on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket. The telescope aims to deliver unprecedented deep, panoramic views of the cosmos, transforming our understanding of the universe.

title: NASA Welcomes Jordan as 63rd Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Jordan signed the Artemis Accords, becoming the 63rd signatory nation committed to responsible space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Jordan's participation during a ceremony at NASA headquarters.

title: Liquid Lifeline: NASA Tech Could Create IV Fluid In Space
summary: NASA is developing technology to produce IV fluid in space, which could be crucial for long-duration crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit. This innovation would reduce reliance on pre-packaged supplies and address medical emergencies during deep-space travel.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission two weeks prior.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on Starship's Block 3 variant at the Cape, with hopes to launch it this year. The first flight of Block 3 is approaching as preparations continue.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and progress of the program.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled across four countries using five different launch vehicles during the week of April 20. This highlights a busy period for global space activities.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the failed launch of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for AST SpaceMobile's satellite broadband ambitions.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, marking a milestone for the partially reusable launch vehicle.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an Astronaut Flight Suit and a Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality for future space missions. The company is developing commercial space station infrastructure.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This marks a significant milestone for the program following the successful return of Orion.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March. The month was marked by high activity aboard the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over the past 50 years. This publication highlights key milestones in ESA's history and contributions to space exploration.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could significantly improve satellite servicing and debris removal operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on 8–9 June in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April–May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement was strategically chosen.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution videos of the Sun's corona. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel three to four times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency’s technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology. It highlights improvements in precision and efficiency for satellite operations.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The experiment showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials with greater efficiency in microgravity compared to Earth's gravity.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to improve scalability and reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The initiative is part of Astra's strategy to enhance its rocket launch capabilities.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Former President Trump has nominated two space industry executives for key military space roles, pending Senate confirmation. The appointments aim to bolster U.S. military space capabilities.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, potentially powering future Moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: The article compares the build costs of orbital and terrestrial data centers, including opportunity costs for terrestrial delays. It presents an analysis using models to evaluate these expenses.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has raised €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have vowed to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, citing concerns that the cuts would hinder the agency's ability to meet presidential directives. The statement was made in response to a budget proposal that lawmakers believe is inadequate for NASA's mission.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships and suggests that the Navy could assist in tracking satellites. It highlights a potential mutual benefit between satellite tracking and naval operations.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has raised €25.7 million in a Series A funding round. The company plans to expand its operational services and develop a full architecture for commercial, institutional, and defense applications.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, initially announced in January 2025, is an ambitious US missile defense program that has attracted significant industry interest. Companies are positioning themselves to compete for contracts related to this program.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year through its CLPS program, creating opportunities for various space industry companies beyond just lunar-focused ones.

title: GomSpace to Establish New Ukrainian Joint Venture for Sovereign Comms
summary: GomSpace, a Danish satellite company, is establishing a new joint venture in Ukraine to develop sovereign satellite communications capabilities. The partnership is with Ukrainian firm Stetman.

title: Rocket Lab Introduces Next-Generation Star Tracker Component
summary: Rocket Lab has unveiled its next-generation High-Performance Star Tracker (ST-HP) component, designed to support longer-duration spacecraft missions. This expansion strengthens the company's offerings in space navigation technology.

title: FCC Rejects Requests for Sharing in MSS Spectrum, Maintaining Exclusive Rights
summary: The FCC rejected requests from SpaceX and other operators to share certain MSS spectrum bands, maintaining exclusive licensing rights. The decision was formalized in an order issued on Thursday.

title: SDA’s Need for Speed Pushes Startups for Results
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is pausing launches until May to address technology hurdles, supply chain challenges, and balance speed with results. Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo emphasized the need for startups to deliver under these strategic delays.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to deploy ultra-compact teleports at Equinx's data centers to support the ground infrastructure for its Meridian constellation. This partnership aims to enhance SpinLaunch's space launch operations.

title: Iridium Continues IoT Subscriber Growth in Q1, Desch Talks NTN Direct Launch and Spectrum
summary: Iridium reported a 2% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 2026, driven by continued growth in IoT subscribers. CEO Matt Desch discussed plans for NTN direct launch and spectrum discussions.

title: Univity Raises Further Funding for VLEO Constellation for Europe
summary: French startup Univity secured a 27 million euro ($32 million) Series A funding round to develop two demonstration satellites for a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) connectivity constellation in Europe.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: U.S. officials criticize the European Union's proposed Space Act, calling it a complicated overreach. The act aims to create a unified regulatory framework for space activities within the EU.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Jetstar Airways, an Australian low-cost airline, has chosen Viasat's Amara solution for in-flight connectivity on its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed on 11 Jetstar aircraft.

title: SSC Space and Kuva Space to Collaborate in Nordic Region
summary: SSC Space and Kuva Space have announced a regional collaboration to enhance space-based capabilities across the Nordic region's space value chain. The partnership aims to strengthen various sectors within the space industry.
